facade_colour: dark red-brown brick with buff/cream brick quoins, string courses, and window surrounds
roof_type: Gable with prominent hose/clock tower rising above

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: dark red-brown brick with buff/cream brick quoins, string courses, and window surrounds
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Well maintained. Dichromatic brickwork in excellent condition. Clock tower appears intact. Sign reading 'NO 8 HOSE STATION' visible at ground level.
photo_observations.window_details: Upper floors have segmentally arched window openings with buff brick voussoirs. Second floor has a prominent round (oculus) window at centre. Third floor (tower level) has paired arched windows. Ground floor has rectangular windows with buff brick surrounds.
photo_observations.door_details: Red painted doors with arched transoms. Original fire station apparatus bay doors likely converted.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Buff brick corbelling and decorative brick cornice at eaves, stepped gable details
photo_observations.decorative_details: Elaborate dichromatic brickwork throughout. Buff brick quoins, string courses, window surrounds and voussoirs contrast with red-brown field brick. Prominent clock tower with clock face. Canadian flag mounted on tower. Stepped/Dutch gable parapet with decorative brick corbelling. Round oculus window on second floor centre.
photo_observations.roof_type_observed: Gable with prominent hose/clock tower rising above
photo_observations.signage_observed: 'NO 8 HOSE STATION' painted on facade, Canadian flag
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Appears well-preserved with minimal visible alterations. Red-painted doors may be modern replacements.
photo_observations.notes: Landmark Victorian fire station with distinctive clock/hose tower. Excellent example of dichromatic brickwork. Two photos available; the closer shot (145746993) shows ground-floor details including round oculus window and decorative brick surrounds more clearly.

notes: Landmark Victorian fire station with distinctive clock/hose tower. Excellent example of dichromatic brickwork. Two photos available; the closer shot (145746993) shows ground-floor details including round oculus window and decorative brick surrounds more clearly.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a gable with prominent hose/clock tower rising above, consistent with its house-form, row typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 3, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 2 principal entrances.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include string courses, quoins, cornice.

facade_description: Three-storey former fire station in dark red-brown brick with buff/cream brick quoins, string courses, and window surrounds with a gable with prominent hose/clock tower rising above roof. Features include simple bargeboards, string courses, quoins and a decorative cornice. Appears well-preserved with minimal visible alterations. Red-painted doors may be modern replacements. Constructed 1889-1903.
